vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence provided. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or have suffered total systemic loss.

inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil is devoid of Pothos.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Uniformly dark, appears to be dry or low-moisture substrate.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The presence of white, crystalline-like debris (possibly perlite or mineral salt buildup) is noted in the P3/P4 area.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Rapid transition to total loss of visible plant matter.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Stasis. No recovery or new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in any of the registered containers.
vision_report.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-soil object remaining. The lack of any green biomass suggests either a recent clearing of the pots or total senescence.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided image sequence, I have determined that the biome has suffered a catastrophic loss of all registered specimens. The pots, while physically present, contain no identifiable plant life. The 'white rabbit' anchor remains in P3, but the Pothos is missing. The soil surfaces are clear of any sprouts or weeds, indicating a sterile or dormant environment. No recovery has been observed across the 5-day window.
